subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: triangulated across multilateral bodies (IMF Article IV, OECD, World Justice Project, RSF, Transparency International, EU Commission) + Dutch official stats (CBS, DNB) + Dutch press/legal sources (NOvA, NL Times, Free Press Unlimited).

Source-lean: political and crime detail leaned on English-language outlets; balanced with native Dutch legal/press; deeper NRC/de Volkskrant/NOS reads would further de-skew political_stability and civil_liberties.
Peak political instability (the June 2025 PVV-triggered cabinet collapse) resolved via the Oct 2025 election; a centrist D66-VVD-CDA minority cabinet was sworn in Feb 2026.

economic.notes: A very strong, low-debt fiscal position within the eurozone, sound banks, and a trade-hub economy; near-term energy-shock inflation the main wobble.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~43% GDP, deficit structurally below 3% through 2030; strong now, with ~4% of GDP of age/health/climate spending pressure building by 2050. (IMF Art IV 2026, OECD)

monetary_independence.notes: Eurozone - the ECB is credible and independent; no national monetary control. (ECB, DNB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Eurozone member; the euro is the #2 global reserve currency, a modest shared benefit. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: 2025 CPI ~3.3%, HICP ~2.5-2.8% spring 2026 but projected up on energy; running above the euro-area average on tight labor/wages. (CBS, EC)

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalized majors (ING, Rabobank, ABN AMRO); solid DNB supervision; high household mortgage debt the structural watch-item. (DNB, IMF FSAP)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Rotterdam is Europe's #1 container hub, but 2025 throughput slipped and exports lagged on weak EU competitiveness; Groningen gas permanently shut (now an LNG/pipeline importer); stable policy, no resource curse. (Port of Rotterdam, CBS)

institutional.notes: Excellent baseline (uncompromised statistics, top-tier press freedom), with a mild rule-of-law erosion trend flagged by domestic and EU bodies.

rule_of_law.notes: WJP #9/143, but the EU 2025 Rule of Law Report and the Dutch bar (NOvA) flag judicial-appointment/prosecution-autonomy concerns and asylum-emergency laws straining constitutional norms. (WJP, EU Commission, advocatenorde.nl)

statistical_integrity.notes: CBS is independent, uncompromised, and high-quality. (CBS, Eurostat)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Professional and capable; nitrogen/housing permitting paralysis exposes delivery-capacity strain. (OECD 2025)

press_freedom.notes: RSF #2 globally (2026); caveat: rising journalist threats and SLAPPs. (RSF, Free Press Unlimited)

political_social.notes: A functioning (if fragmented, minority) government after migration-driven instability, with a tightening migration climate, social polarization, and a housing-strained ageing population.

political_stability.notes: Two cabinets collapsed over migration (2023, June 2025), but the Oct 2025 election and a Feb 2026 D66-VVD-CDA cabinet restored a functioning (if minority, 66/150) government; fragmentation persists. (CBS, NL Times)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong baseline; recent asylum/migration restrictions test equal-treatment norms. (Amnesty NL, NOvA)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: The Dutch-American Friendship Treaty (DAFT) gives US citizens a uniquely easy lawful route (~EUR 4,500 business setup), alongside the highly-skilled-migrant scheme and EU Blue Card. But the 30% expat tax ruling was cut, asylum/migration rules hardened, and naturalization may move 5->10 years; the skilled/DAFT path stays open while the overall climate tightens. (gov.nl, Dentons)

social_cohesion.notes: Sharp migration polarization and nitrogen/farmer protests (a court ordered enforced cuts with penalty risk); a high baseline of trust cushions it. (CBS)

demographics.notes: Population ~18.1M but deaths now exceed births; growth is entirely migration-driven; over-65s outnumber under-20s; a ~450k housing shortage worsens it. (CBS)

geopolitical.notes: A reliable NATO/EU core member; tail exposure via Russian hybrid/cyber activity and as an organized-crime logistics vector.

alliance_reliability.notes: NATO/EU core; hosted the June 2025 Hague Summit and committed to 5% (3.5%+1.5%) by 2035. (NATO, gov.nl)

conflict_involvement.notes: No direct conflict, but Russian hybrid/cyber pressure and Rotterdam/Schiphol as a cocaine/organized-crime vector; tail exposure as a NATO member. (NATO, OC Index)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: EU sanctions regime; no domestic capital controls; free capital mobility. (EU, DNB)

physical_practical.notes: Top-tier healthcare and infrastructure and world-class water management, against severe structural sea-level exposure held back by exceptional defenses.

climate.notes: Best-in-world flood engineering and top readiness offset extreme sea-level/subsidence near-term; AMOC-slowdown plus accelerating SLR are the long-term tail. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AMOC/SLR)

healthcare.notes: Universal mandatory-insurance system; very low unmet need; top-decile outcomes at ~10% of GDP. (OECD Health at a Glance 2025)

infrastructure.notes: Top-tier ports, rail, broadband, cycling; watch-items are electricity-grid congestion and housing. (OECD, CBS)

crime_safety.notes: Homicide ~0.7/100k and violent crime down over 2010-2024, though the long decline stalled post-2018 and organized-crime violence is concentrated. (CBS, OC Index)

disaster_insurance.notes: Strong response capacity; a known flood-insurance gap (heavy reliance on state defenses, limited private flood cover). (DNB, EC)

summary: World-class institutions, infrastructure, and water management, a very strong low-debt fiscal position within the eurozone, and a uniquely favorable lawful residency route for US citizens via the Dutch-American Friendship Treaty. The offsetting drags are serial migration-driven government instability (now past its peak), a tightening migration climate, an acute housing shortage with ageing demographics, and severe long-run sea-level exposure held back only by exceptional flood defenses.
